problems.title: Team Skills Analyzer
problems.description:
You are managing two project teams and need to analyze their combined skill sets. Given two sets of skills (one for each team), your program should:
1. Print the combined (union) skills of both teams — all unique skills across both teams.
2. Print the common (intersection) skills — skills that both teams share.
3. Print the skills unique to Team A — skills that only Team A has.
4. Print the skills unique to Team B — skills that only Team B has.

Each result should be printed as a sorted list (alphabetically) on a separate line.
constraints: Each team has between 1 and 10 skills.
Skill names are single words (no spaces), all lowercase.
Skills within each team are unique.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Skills of Team A separated by spaces.
Line 2: Skills of Team B separated by spaces.
problems.outputFormat:
Line 1: Combined: followed by sorted combined skills, separated by commas and a space.
Line 2: Common: followed by sorted common skills. If none, print Common: none
Line 3: Only Team A: followed by sorted unique Team A skills. If none, print Only Team A: none
Line 4: Only Team B: followed by sorted unique Team B skills. If none, print Only Team B: none
problems.hint:
team_a = set(input().split())
team_b = set(input().split())
combined = sorted(team_a | team_b)
common   = sorted(team_a & team_b)
only_a   = sorted(team_a - team_b)
only_b   = sorted(team_b - team_a)
print('Combined:', ', '.join(combined))
print('Common:',   ', '.join(common)   if common   else 'none')
print('Only Team A:', ', '.join(only_a) if only_a else 'none')
print('Only Team B:', ', '.join(only_b) if only_b else 'none')

steps.text: Read line 1 and convert to a set for Team A using set(input().split()).
Read line 2 and convert to a set for Team B.
Use | for union (combined), & for intersection (common), - for difference (unique to each).
Sort each result using sorted() for alphabetical order.
Join with ', '.join() and print each line. If a result is empty, print 'none' instead.

testCases.label: Example 1
expected: Combined: excel, java, powerbi, python, sql

testCases.label: No skills in common
expected: Combined: go, java, kotlin, python, rust, swift
Only Team A: go, java, python
Only Team B: kotlin, rust, swift

testCases.label: Hidden: One shared, rest unique
expected: Combined: css, django, node, python, react

testCases.label: Hidden: Team A skills all in Team B
expected: Combined: java, node, python, react, sql
Only Team B: node, react, sql

testCases.label: Hidden: More skills, partial overlap
expected: Combined: angular, css, html, java, python, react, sql, vue
Only Team A: css, html, python
Only Team B: angular, react, vue

problems.title: Duplicate Remover and Set Builder
problems.description:
You are building a student attendance tracker. Your program will:
1. Read a list of student names (which may contain duplicates).
2. Convert the list to a set to remove duplicates.
3. Print the number of unique students.
4. Read a name to add to the set.
5. Read a name to discard from the set (should not raise an error if not found).
6. Print the final sorted set of student names.
constraints: The initial list has between 1 and 20 names.
Names are single words, starting with an uppercase letter.
The name to add may or may not already be in the set.
The name to discard may or may not be in the set.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Student names separated by spaces (may contain duplicates).
Line 2: A single name to add to the set.
Line 3: A single name to discard from the set.
problems.outputFormat:
Line 1: Unique count: followed by the number of unique students (after removing duplicates, before add/discard).
Line 2: Final list: followed by the sorted final student names, separated by commas and a space.
problems.hint:
names = input().split()
add_name     = input().strip()
discard_name = input().strip()
unique = set(names)
print('Unique count:', len(unique))
unique.add(add_name)
unique.discard(discard_name)  # discard won't raise error if not found
print('Final list:', ', '.join(sorted(unique)))

steps.text: Read line 1, split by spaces — you get a list that may have duplicates.
Convert to a set immediately to remove duplicates.
Print 'Unique count:' with the size of the set NOW (before add/discard).
Read the name to add and use .add() on the set.
Read the name to discard and use .discard() — this is safe even if the name isn't in the set.
Sort and join the final set with ', '.join(sorted(unique)) and print.

testCases.label: Example 1
inputs: Alice Bob Alice Charlie Bob Alice

testCases.label: Example 2
expected: Final list: Ana, Juan, Maria, Pedro

testCases.label: Hidden: No duplicates in input
expected: Final list: Alice, Charlie, Dana, Eve

testCases.label: Hidden: Two duplicates, add new name
Hidden: Discard name not in set
expected: Final list: Alice, Bob, Charlie, Diana

testCases.label: Hidden: Many duplicates, small final set
inputs: Anna Anna Bob Bob Carlos Carlos

problems.title: Course Prerequisite Checker
problems.description:
A university system needs to check if a student has completed all the prerequisite courses for enrollment in an advanced program. Your program should:
1. Read the required courses (prerequisites).
2. Read the completed courses by the student.
3. Check if the student has completed all prerequisites (i.e., if the required courses are a subset of the completed courses).
4. If yes, print a success message.
5. If no, print the missing courses (sorted alphabetically).
6. Finally, print the symmetric difference — courses that are in one set but not the other. Print them sorted.
constraints: Each set has between 1 and 10 course names.
Course names are single words (no spaces), all lowercase.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Required courses separated by spaces.
Line 2: Completed courses separated by spaces.
problems.outputFormat:
Line 1: Eligible: yes if all prerequisites are met, otherwise Eligible: no
Line 2 (only if not eligible): Missing: followed by the sorted missing courses, separated by commas and a space.
Last line: Non-overlapping: followed by the sorted symmetric difference. If none, print Non-overlapping: none
problems.hint:
required  = set(input().split())
completed = set(input().split())
eligible  = required.issubset(completed)
print('Eligible: yes' if eligible else 'Eligible: no')
if not eligible:
    missing = sorted(required - completed)
    print('Missing:', ', '.join(missing))
sym_diff = sorted(required.symmetric_difference(completed))
print('Non-overlapping:', ', '.join(sym_diff) if sym_diff else 'none')

steps.text: Read both lines and convert each to a set.
Use required.issubset(completed) to check if all prerequisites are done.
Print 'Eligible: yes' or 'Eligible: no' based on the check.
If not eligible, compute required - completed to find missing courses and print them sorted.
Use .symmetric_difference() to get courses in one set but not both — print sorted, or 'none' if empty.

testCases.label: Hidden: All required completed, no extras
